prerequisites: An Astra DB account at astra.datastax.com with a serverless vector database deployed.
The database API endpoint, found under Database Details on the database Overview tab in the Astra Portal (used for the indexing config).
An Astra DB application token with at least Database Administrator access to the target database.
An API key from an embedding provider (OpenAI, Cohere, or Azure OpenAI) unless you use a no-embedding option.
A target collection name and keyspace (namespace) in Astra DB to write vectors into.

authMethods.name: Astra DB application token
authMethods.summary: Set indexing.astra_db_app_token to an Astra DB application token (prefixed AstraCS:). Generate it from the database Overview tab in the Astra Portal via Generate Token; this creates a Database Administrator role scoped to that database. The token is shown only once, so copy and store it securely.

authMethods.name: OpenAI embedding API key
authMethods.summary: When the embedding provider is OpenAI, set embedding.openai_key to your OpenAI API key. Used to generate vector embeddings of the configured text fields before writing them to Astra DB.

authMethods.name: Cohere embedding API key
authMethods.summary: When the embedding provider is Cohere, set embedding.cohere_key to your Cohere API key. Used to generate vector embeddings of the configured text fields.

authMethods.name: Azure OpenAI embedding API key
authMethods.summary: When the embedding provider is Azure OpenAI, set embedding.api_key to your Azure OpenAI API key. Used to generate vector embeddings of the configured text fields.

setupSteps.title: Create a serverless vector database in Astra
setupSteps.body: Sign in at astra.datastax.com and create a serverless database. Open the database, go to the Overview tab, and note the API endpoint shown under Database Details; you will provide this in the indexing configuration alongside a target collection and keyspace.

setupSteps.title: Generate an Astra DB application token
setupSteps.body: On the database Overview tab, under Database Details, click Generate Token. Optionally add a description and set an expiration, then click Generate Token and copy the value (prefixed AstraCS:). This token, scoped as Database Administrator for that database, is shown only once. Provide it as indexing.astra_db_app_token.

setupSteps.title: Configure the indexing section
setupSteps.body: In the indexing config, set astra_db_app_token (your application token), astra_db_endpoint (the database API endpoint), the target collection, and the keyspace (namespace) where vectors will be written.

setupSteps.title: Configure the embedding section
setupSteps.body: Choose an embedding provider and supply its key: embedding.openai_key for OpenAI, embedding.cohere_key for Cohere, or embedding.api_key for Azure OpenAI. The embedding model converts the selected text fields into vectors before loading.

setupSteps.title: Configure the processing section and options
setupSteps.body: In the processing config, set the chunk size for splitting records, the text fields to embed, and the metadata fields to store. Optionally enable omit_raw_text to exclude the original text from the destination and reduce storage.

sources.title: DataStax: Manage application tokens (Astra DB Serverless)
DataStax: Integrate Airbyte with Astra DB Serverless
